channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Powerful US storms bring threats ranging from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms tore roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma and threatened more communities across the country Tuesday with weather ranging from fire in the Southern High Plains to blizzards in the Midwest.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Red Robin considering closing dozens of 'underperforming' restaurants
item.description: Red Robin, which first opened in Seattle in 1969, currently has 498 restaurants across the United States and in British Columbia, Canada.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson tells GOP lawmakers to skip town halls after an onslaught of protests
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Speaker Mike Johnson is encouraging Republican lawmakers to skip town halls that have been filled with protesters decrying the Trump administration's slashing of federal government, echoing the president's claims that the demonstration's are fueled by professional protesters.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hateful message left on Ohio restaurant receipt results in outpouring of support, staff says
item.description: The public is rallying behind a central Ohio Mexican restaurant after a customer left a hateful message instead of a tip.
item.dc:creator: Anna Hoffman, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans advised to avoid in-person town halls amid protests
item.description: House Republicans are being encouraged to avoid in-person town halls in favor of phone and livestreamed versions, advice that comes after constituents and activists rocked recent GOP events by expressing outrage about the Trump administration’s dramatic moves to reshape the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: 9 states report measles cases as outbreaks grow
item.description: One of the most contagious viruses in the world is spreading around the United States. So far in 2025 there have been three distinct measles outbreaks and at least 164 cases reported to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention coming from nine states.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Pope Francis off mechanical ventilation, now on supplemental oxygen
item.description: Pope Francis had stabilized enough Tuesday after two respiratory crises to be taken off noninvasive mechanical ventilation and receive just high flows of supplemental oxygen
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer mice with thick hair like the extinct woolly mammoth
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Extinction is still forever, but scientists at the biotech company Colossal Biosciences are trying what they say is the next best thing to restoring ancient beasts — genetically engineering living animals with qualities to resemble extinct species like the woolly mammoth.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google makes it easier to remove your personal info
item.description: Google is making removing your personal information from search results way easier. Now, when you search for yourself and see a link that contains information like your email, address or phone number, you can request a takedown in seconds.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Republicans target 4 'sanctuary' cities as Trump pushes mass deportations
item.description: Republicans in Congress are taking aim at four cities over their policies limiting cooperation with immigration enforcement under President Donald Trump's administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FAA investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Monday it is investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How to watch Trump address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to address a joint session of Congress for the first time during his second term on Tuesday, outlining the goals of his administration and how he views the state of the country.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court will hear from US gun makers sued by Mexico for $10 billion
item.description: The Supreme Court will hear arguments in a $10 billion lawsuit Mexico filed against top U.S. firearm manufacturers, alleging their business practices have fueled cartel gun violence there.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis no longer requires mechanical ventilation after respiratory crisis
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis had stabilized enough Tuesday after two respiratory crises to be taken off noninvasive mechanical ventilation and receive just high flows of supplemental oxygen, as he continued his more than two-week long fight against double pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US tariffs on Canada, Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, PAUL WISEMAN and ROB GILLIES Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York prison guards fired for ignoring deal to end strike, thousands set to lose health insurance
item.description: New York officials have started to fire state prison guards who failed to comply with a deal to end their illegal labor strike, which is now in its third week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah mommy blogger Ruby Franke's power, public image allowed child abuse to go 'unchecked': expert
item.description: Disgraced parenting blogger and mom of six Ruby Franke's "power" and public image" allowed her crimes against her children to go "unchecked," according to a defense attorney.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Powerful US storms bring threats ranging from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: Powerful storms were threatening communities across the country with weather ranging from fire in the Southern High Plains to blizzards in the Midwest.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin And Jack Brook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WGN 9

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Cat food recalled over risk of bird flu weeks after pets euthanized
item.description: The Wild Coast LLC product 16oz and 24oz frozen Boneless Free Range Chicken Formula raw pet food for cats was distributed to pet food retailers in Washington State and Oregon. The product is frozen in a small round white plastic container with a green label.
item.dc:creator: Erin Yudt, KRON4

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: The Education Department sent out an email to staffers on Friday saying if they quit by Monday at midnight, they will receive a $25,000 cash payout.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Crews from across South Carolina help battle Carolina Forest-area wildfires
item.description: Regional and state fire rescue crews on Monday continued to battle the Carolina Forest-area wildfires, according to Horry County Fire Rescue.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, Fox 5

item.title: This rare bean may be your future coffee as warming climate hurts crops
item.description: As leading coffee-producing countries struggle to grow crops in drier, less reliable weather, prices have soared to the highest in decades and the industry is scrambling for solutions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Magnitude 4.5 earthquake strikes in Washington state
item.description: A “notable” earthquake registering at a preliminary magnitude of 4.5 struck Washington State on Monday morning, according to the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Crews battle wildfires in North and South Carolina amid dry conditions and gusty winds
item.description: Crews battled wildfires in North and South Carolina on Sunday amid dry conditions and gusty winds as residents were forced to evacuate in some areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Lethal injection, electrocution and now firing squads. A look at US execution methods
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — South Carolina is preparing this week to execute a man by firing squad, a capital punishment method that hasn’t been used in the U.S. in nearly 15 years.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at the Oscars
item.description: "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as European leaders assembled in London to discuss ending the war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs order designating English as the official language of the US
item.description: President Donald Trump signed on Saturday an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: David Johansen, singer from the seminal punk band the New York Dolls, dies at 75
item.description: David Johansen, the wiry, gravelly-voiced singer and last surviving member of the glam and protopunk band the New York Dolls who later performed as his campy, pompadoured alter ego, Buster Poindexter, has died. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New charge filed against son of Buc-ee's co-founder
item.description: Mitchell Wasek, 29, is being charged with a third-degree felony offense of possession or promotion of child pornography.
item.dc:creator: Dalton Huey, WFLA, NBC
